decks.notes: This deck focuses on quickly playing powerful artifacts to disrupt the opponent and take control of the game. It aims to generate mana quickly with Mishra's Workshop and other artifacts, while also using cards like Glaring Fleshraker and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain to apply pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Trinisphere and Chalice of the Void helps to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while Mystic Forge and Key cards like Time Vault and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional versatility and win conditions. The deck excels at overwhelming opponents with fast plays and efficient artifacts.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's gameplay by using artifacts like Null Rod and Sphere of Resistance, while also using powerful mana sources like Mishra's Workshop and Ancient Tomb to quickly deploy threats like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Karn, the Great Creator. The deck aims to control the game by utilizing powerful artifacts and land destruction, while also being able to generate mana advantage through cards like Tolarian Academy and Sol Ring. The strategy revolves around setting up a strong board presence and overwhelming the opponent with fast, aggressive plays.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game using counter spells, removal, and hand disruption while leveraging powerful card advantage engines like Lórien Revealed and Dig Through Time. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan, protect its own threats, and ultimately grind out a win through attrition. It also utilizes the graveyard as a resource with cards like Nihil Spellbomb and Snapcaster Mage. The mana base is streamlined to support the deck's color requirements and utilize utility lands like Wasteland and Urza's Saga.

This deck focuses on using cards like Force of Will, Force of Negation, and other counterspells to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also includes powerful card draw and mana acceleration cards like Ancestral Recall, Time Walk, and the Moxen to maintain card advantage and stay ahead in mana development. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional value and recursion in the late game. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through incremental advantage and superior card quality.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill your graveyard with cards like Stinkweed Imp and Golgari Thug, allowing you to trigger dredge abilities and bring back creatures from the graveyard. Utilizing cards like Bazaar of Baghdad and Serum Powder help you quickly find the pieces you need for your combo. Creeping Chill and Prized Amalgam provide additional value when they are brought back from the graveyard. Cards like Force of Will and Force of Negation protect your combo pieces while cards like Grief disrupt your opponent's hand. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm and out-value your opponent through graveyard shenanigans.

This Vintage Eldrazi Aggro deck focuses on fast mana acceleration and powerful Eldrazi creatures to quickly overwhelm opponents. The deck includes a mix of mana rocks like Black Lotus and Moxen, along with lands like Eldrazi Temple and Eye of Ugin to cast Eldrazi threats ahead of curve. Support cards like Sensei's Divining Top and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain help control the board while also providing card advantage. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying large threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Mutated Cultist to close out the game quickly.

The deck focuses on combining aggressive creatures with disruptive hate cards to slow down the opponent and gain control of the game quickly. By using creatures like Clarion Conqueror and White Plume Adventurer to apply pressure, while also utilizing hate cards like Archon of Emeria and Chancellor of the Annex to disrupt the opponent's game plan, the deck aims to establish an early advantage and maintain it throughout the game. The inclusion of mana acceleration and disruptive artifacts like Void Mirror and Chalice of the Void further supports the strategy of overwhelming the opponent with an efficient, disruptive aggro strategy.

The key focus of this deck is to utilize the Oath of Druids strategy to quickly put powerful creatures into play, while also controlling the game through a mix of countermagic and disruptive spells. The deck aims to ramp up quickly with mana acceleration cards like Black Lotus and Wrenn and Six, then lock down the opponent's resources with cards like Wasteland and Null Rod. Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Oko, Thief of Crowns provide additional control elements, while Gaea's Blessing helps to recycle key cards and maintain consistency in the deck.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan while maintaining a strong board presence. It uses a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive permanents like Null Rod and Pithing Needle to handle threats. The deck also includes powerful draw spells and tutors to find key cards like Ancestral Recall or Demonic Tutor. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent's strategy, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent for victory.

The deck focuses on using aggressive MUD (Metalworker, Urza's Bauble, Dreamstone Hedron) creatures and artifacts to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It also includes ways to disrupt the opponent's strategies, such as Chalice of the Void and Grafdigger's Cage. The deck aims to control the game's tempo, generate mana quickly, and take advantage of artifacts like Mishra's Workshop and Mystic Forge to create a powerful board presence. The key to success lies in efficiently deploying threats and disrupting the opponent's game plan while maintaining a strong mana base.

This deck aims to assemble a powerful combination of artifacts, mana acceleration, and card draw to generate a game-winning advantage. Key cards such as Coveted Jewel, Paradoxical Outcome, and Phyrexian Metamorph are used to create a high amount of mana and card advantage, while control elements like Force of Will and disruptive creatures like Lodestone Golem help to protect the combo. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a massive board state and overwhelming resources, leading to victory through sheer power and advantage.

This deck focuses on setting up a powerful winning combination using the Doomsday card, along with various control and card draw elements to protect and set up the combo. The main goal is to efficiently stack your deck with the necessary cards to win the game, while disrupting your opponent's plans with counter spells and hand disruption. Lurrus of the Dream-Den can provide additional value by allowing you to replay your smaller permanents from the graveyard. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo and win decisively.

This deck focuses on playing disruptive creatures to slow down the opponent while also ramping up its own mana production. The goal is to control the game by hindering the opponent's ability to cast spells efficiently, while also utilizing powerful hate cards to disrupt their strategy. By applying early pressure and impeding the opponent's game plan, this deck aims to gain a tempo advantage and close out the game quickly with its aggressive creatures.

Utilize artifacts and powerful mana acceleration to quickly assemble a combo involving Coveted Jewel and other key cards, allowing for explosive plays and overwhelming advantage. Key cards like Tezzeret, Grim Monolith, and Paradoxical Outcome help to fuel the combo engine, while cards like Force of Will provide protection. This deck aims to outpace opponents and disrupt their strategies, while generating massive card advantage and mana advantage to secure victory. The synergy between multiple artifacts and spells creates a complex web of interactions that can quickly overwhelm opponents.

The deck revolves around generating lots of mana quickly and efficiently through cards like Mishra's Workshop, Grim Monolith, and The One Ring in order to assemble the Coveted Jewel combo. Through cards like Paradoxical Outcome and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain, the player can generate card advantage and manipulate the board state to their advantage. The deck also includes disruption cards like Force of Will and Sink into Stupor to control the game and protect the combo pieces. Ultimately, the goal is to win the game by using the Coveted Jewel combo to overpower the opponent.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combination of artifacts to generate mana and draw cards while controlling the opponent's board with counter spells. The key strategy involves using cards like Tinker to find powerful artifacts like Coveted Jewel, Time Vault, and Karn, the Great Creator, to create an overwhelming advantage. By leveraging powerful mana acceleration and card advantage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of powerful artifacts and disruptive spells.
